sections.title: Mission
sections.summary:
What matters now: the only live decisions are the pending approvals—draft the troop-parent message, the vendor package, and the Rebekah approval flow—while the calendar pressure is still a few days out.  
What can wait: front-door unlock is high-risk and needs second factor, and the May 15–17 events only need light prep until the week of.  
Clean next move: clear the medium-risk approvals first, then handle the high-risk door only with explicit second-factor confirmation.
details: The biggest cross-domain effect today is timing: “dawn-protocol” is active, but the calendar is mostly later-day family logistics, so the immediate win is using this quiet morning window to front-load Chris’s brief, Chapter 4 tightening, and workshop-bracket cleanup before the household schedule gets noisy. That also reduces collision risk with Rebekah’s coordination load and keeps Caleb/Anna support from getting squeezed by your own prep work later.

sections.summary:

What can wait: the front door unlock is high-risk and needs second factor, and the calendar review/email reading requests can sit unless they’re blocking something urgent.

details: First light today is clear: no immediate calendar pressure until Anna’s observation on May 13, so the main second-order effect is that Chris can use the morning to tighten prep without family-calendar conflict. What can wait: the wealth strategy threads and old planning ideas can sit; they’re already converging on one answer, so no need to reopen them today.
